product_overview: Text2Form.ai is an AI-first form and quiz builder that converts natural-language prompts (and optional uploaded documents) into shareable web forms and quizzes. Generated forms can be edited, published, submitted publicly, and analyzed in a real-time dashboard.
core_goals: Enable users to create professional forms/quizzes instantly from plain-English prompts or uploaded documents using Gemini AI.
Support a dual-user model: guest creation (unclaimed forms) and authenticated ownership (Google OAuth via Supabase) with claiming.
Protect quiz integrity by stripping correct answers for non-owners and provide server-side quiz scoring.
Provide an intuitive editor to refine AI-generated schemas and an analytics dashboard with submission charts and tables.
Make sharing simple (copyable links) and ensure resilient handling of AI/IO/database edge cases.

features.name: Form Generation (AI)
features.description: Create a structured form schema from a natural-language prompt and optional uploaded files using the AI generation API, then redirect to the form editor.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Fill text prompt or upload file -> Click generate button -> See loading spinner -> Receive generation response with formId -> Be redirected to /dashboard/[id]/edit -> See generated form fields
Navigate to / -> Click generate button without prompt or files -> See generation error (data-testid="generation-error")

Navigate to / -> Upload supported files (pdf/docx/txt/png/jpg) -> Fill text prompt -> Click generate button -> See loading spinner -> Receive generation response with formId -> Be redirected to /dashboard/[id]/edit -> See generated form fields derived from uploaded documents
Navigate to / -> Fill text prompt -> Click generate button -> AI returns invalid JSON -> See error toast 'AI returned invalid format' and remain on /

features.name: Form Editor
features.description: Editable UI for refining a generated form: add/remove/reorder fields, change types/properties, toggle quiz mode, and save updates to the database.
user_flows: Navigate to /dashboard/[id]/edit -> See generated form title, description, and fields -> Modify form field properties -> Add new form fields -> Remove form fields -> Click Save Form button -> If form is guest (unowned) be redirected to /f/[id]?success=true -> See success sharing UI

Navigate to /dashboard/[id]/edit while authenticated and owner -> See generated form title, description, and fields -> Modify form field properties -> Click Save Form button -> Be redirected to /dashboard/[id] -> See updated analytics header
Navigate to /dashboard/[id]/edit for a form owned by another user -> Attempt to edit fields -> See access denied toast 'Access denied: You do not own this form' -> Be redirected to /dashboard
Navigate to /dashboard/[id]/edit -> Click Save Form button -> Server fails to update (DB error) -> See save error toast and remain on /dashboard/[id]/edit

features.name: Public Form Submission
features.description: Public-facing form page that renders the DynamicForm from the stored schema, accepts submissions, posts answers to the submission API, and shows thank-you or quiz-score states.
user_flows: Navigate to /f/[id] -> See form title and description (data-testid="form-title") -> Fill out the form fields -> Click Submit button -> See loading spinner on submit -> Receive success response -> See 'Thank You! Your response has been recorded.'
Navigate to /f/[id] (quiz form) -> Fill out the form fields with answer selections -> Click Submit button -> Receive score response -> See quiz score display 'X / Y' on the page
Navigate to /f/invalid-id -> See styled error 'Form not found' (data-testid="form-error") -> Click Back to Home link -> Navigate to /
Navigate to /f/[id] -> Leave required field blank -> Click Submit button -> See inline validation error and destructive-styled banner preventing submission

features.name: Submissions Analytics (Dashboard Detail)
features.description: Owner-facing analytics page showing form metadata, total responses, average score (for quizzes), response distribution bar chart, and a submissions table.
user_flows: Navigate to /dashboard/[id] while authenticated as owner -> See form title & description -> View charts (AnalyticsChart) -> Access submission records table (SubmissionsTable) -> Inspect individual submission rows
Navigate to /dashboard/[id] -> See 'stat-total-responses' and 'stat-total-fields' stat cards -> Click View Live button (data-testid="view-form-link") -> Open /f/[id] in a new tab
Navigate to /dashboard/[id] while not owner -> See Access Denied state with lock icon and message 'Only the creator of this form can view its dashboard' -> Remain on page with restricted UI
Navigate to /dashboard/[id] -> No chartable fields present or no submissions -> See 'No data available yet' or 'No submissions yet' empty state in chart/table

features.name: Dashboard Index (My Vault)
features.description: Authenticated user's list of forms with quick actions to view analytics, edit forms, or open the public form; server-side redirect prevents unauthenticated access.
user_flows: Navigate to /dashboard while authenticated -> See 'My Vault' header -> See grid of form cards with response counts and action buttons -> Click '+ New Form' -> Navigate to /
Navigate to /dashboard while unauthenticated -> Server redirects to / -> See home page and login CTA
Navigate to /dashboard -> Click a card's Edit Form action -> Navigate to /dashboard/[id]/edit -> Edit form
Navigate to /dashboard -> Click a card's View Public Link action -> Open /f/[id] in a new tab

features.name: Authentication & Form Claiming
features.description: Google OAuth via Supabase enables users to sign in, claim guest forms created earlier, and manage owned forms in the dashboard.
user_flows: On /f/[id]?success=true click 'Sign in to Save' -> Initiate Google OAuth consent -> Complete OAuth flow -> Supabase sets session and claims form -> Be redirected to /dashboard -> See claimed form in My Vault
On any page click Login button (navbar) -> Initiate Google OAuth -> OAuth fails or callback error -> Be redirected to /?error=auth-callback-failed -> See error toast
Authenticated user signs out via navbar sign-out -> Client calls supabase.auth.signOut() -> Redirect to / -> See logged-out navbar state

features.name: File Upload System (Document Context for AI)
features.description: Drag-and-drop multi-file uploader on the home page that accepts PDFs, DOCX, TXT, and images to provide AI context for form generation.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Drag and drop up to 3 supported files into FileUploader -> See file preview cards with type badges and file size -> Fill text prompt -> Click generate button -> Be redirected to /dashboard/[id]/edit with AI-derived fields from uploaded documents
Navigate to / -> Attempt to drop more than 3 files -> Dropzone disables additional files and shows max-files reached state
Navigate to / -> Drop unsupported file type (e.g., .exe) -> See rejection message and no preview card added -> Prompt remains available for generation

features.name: Clipboard Sharing (Copy Link)
features.description: Quick copy-to-clipboard buttons on the dashboard and public form pages to copy the shareable /f/[id] URL with user feedback.
user_flows: Navigate to /dashboard/[id] -> Click Share form button (data-testid="copy-link-button") -> Clipboard copies {origin}/f/{formId} -> See 'Copied!' feedback for 2s
Navigate to /f/[id] -> Click Share Link button -> Clipboard copies current URL -> See 'Copied!' feedback for 2s
Navigate to /dashboard/[id] -> Click Share form button when browser blocks clipboard -> See failure toast and remain on /dashboard/[id]

routes.description: Landing page where users can input text to generate a form.
Dashboard for authenticated users to view forms, submissions, and analytics.
Analytics and details for a specific generated form.
Form Editor where users can edit generated forms before publishing.
Public submission page for users to submit a generated form.

features.name: Form Generation
features.description: AI-powered form generation based on user prompt inputs.
user_interactions: Fill text prompt or upload file
Be redirected to the form editor

features.name: Form Editor
features.description: Interface for users to edit generated forms before publishing.

features.name: Form Submission
features.description: Public interface handling user submissions of forms.

features.name: Submissions Analytics
features.description: Display statistics and charting for submissions.
